document.docAuthor: Mérida-Rivas, Jorge A., Hinojosa-Díaz, Ismael A., Ayala-Barajas, Ricardo, Pozo, Carmen & Vandame, Rémy
document.docTitle: Xylocopa (Neoxylocopa) griswoldi Mérida-Rivas, Hinojosa-Díaz, Ayala-Barajas, Pozo & Vandame, 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Revision of carpenter bees of the subgenus Neoxylocopa Michener (Hymenoptera: Apidae) from Mexico and Mesoamerica

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Revision of carpenter bees of the subgenus Neoxylocopa Michener (Hymenoptera: Apidae) from Mexico and Mesoamerica

heading.emphasis:
Xylocopa ( Neoxylocopa ) griswoldi
Mérida , Hinojosa-Díaz & Ayala sp. nov.

subSubSection.paragraph: Diagnosis . Body length, female, 24.48 mm (22.84–25.50; n=5); male, 25.51 mm (23.95–27.19; n=3). Female: Head without elevated ridges or prominences on frons; ocelli below tangent of the eyes; area between parapsidal line and mesoscutal disc densely punctate (puncture separation one to one and a half puncture diameters); wings dark-brown with purple iridescence, bluish toward base and papillate region ( Fig. 59 ); pubescence evenly black; integument of terga evenly black; T1 densely punctate on disc (punctures separated by one or less puncture diameter), T2 to T4 sparsely punctate (punctures separated by three or four times their diameter) ( Fig. 61 ); length of hairs on terga respect MOD, T2 0.22–0.54, T3 0.28–0.56, T4 0.51–0.84, T5 0.71–1.07. Male: Terga yellowish-orange, without bands; metasoma with yellowish-orange pubescence; posterior tibia internal distal extreme with prominent subapical projection, with curved distal extreme and elevated rounded margin defining a concave area; posterior margin of this process with a laminar margin on almost one third of the posterior margin of the tibia ( Fig. 65 ); length of hairs on terga respect MOD, T2 0.41–0.92, T3 0.73–1.31, T4 0.89–1.31, T5 1.01–1.32; tip of the lateral process of the gonostylus ending oval-shaped (as broad as shaft of the process) ( Fig. 118 ).

Description .
Holotype
: Female : Body length 24.48 mm (22.84–25.50; n=5), length of forewing 21.25 mm (20.40–21.62; n=5), head length 5.77 mm (5.62–5.95; n=5), intertegular distance 7.04 mm (6.38–7.48; n=5), width of T2 11.16 mm (10.43–11.84; n=5). Head : Mandibles bidentate, apical tooth longer and wider than the internal, defined by a notch ( Fig. 57 ). Labrum with three tubercles, the middle narrower than the lateral ones. Ocelli located below the upper margin of the eyes; frontal anterior groove anterior to the middle ocellus well-defined unpunctate, initating with a diameter equal to the width of the ocellus, diminishing to end in a middle elevation between the antennal sockets; pits on areas sublateral to the posterior ocelli of half the diameter of the ocelli. Vertex elevated above the upper margin of the eyes.
Mesosoma : Scutellum
convex in lateral view. Metanotum and propodeum vertical. Posterior tibia with basitibial plate toward the middle region, with two asymetrical spiniform projections with similar width ( Fig. 60 ), with a small notch between them; the anterior projection sharp, the posterior rounded, continuing along the posterior margin. Metasoma : T1 with vertical folds with foveate surface. Color : Integument usually black; holotype and some paratypes with T2 to T4 with reddish integument on the subapical margin. Antennae with distal extreme of scape and pedicel reddish. Wings with iridescence magenta, with some of bluish on the base and papillate region. S1 to S5 with reddish distal margin. Punctures : Head : Clypeus with inferior margin unpunctate; in some paratypes submargin anterior with a small depression; superior área toward subapical margin with dense punctures, a bit more separated in discal area; inferolateral areas with punctures smaller and scattered; supraclipeal area and vertex densely punctate, of similar size as those on clypeus. Genal area with densely punctate on inferior areas, separated by one and a half times or more their diameter on the middle and upper regions.
Mesosoma : Scutum
unpunctate on discal area; between the parapsidal line and discal area with dense punctures, separated by one to one and a half times their diameter; middle anterior region of mesoscutum with dense punctures. Scutellum with few punctures on middle region, in contrast to lateral and posterior regions densely punctate. Metanotum densely punctate, separated by two or more times their diameter, shallower than on scutellum. Mesepisternum and mesoesternal area densely punctate. Tegulae mostly unpunctate, but with dense punctures toward the anterior region. Propodeum with dense superimposed punctures. Metasoma : T1 densely punctate on discal area, separated by 1 time or less their diameter; T2 to T4 with punctures on discal area separated by three or four times their diameter; T5 and T6 densely punctate. S1 to S5 densely punctate on middle to subapical regions; apical margin unpunctate. Pubescence : Mostly black, except on antennae, on the extreme apical of surface anterior of F1 to F10, which has very short dark brown hairs, and on middle distal extreme of T6 which has simple reddish hairs. Head : Vertex and genal area with mixed of simple and branched hairs, the latter shorter than the former.
Mesosoma : In
general with dense pubescence, except on disc of the scutum; the hairs plumose and simple; with some long simple hairs on posterior area of scutellum. Fore legs with long hairs on posterior margin; short hairs on anterior margin of fore, middle, and hind legs. Metasoma : Discal area of T1 with plumose hairs; discal and sublateral areas of T2 to T4 with simple short hairs; T5 with long hairs on discal area; T6 with very dense pubescence; lateral regions of T1 to T6 with long dense and Plumose pubescence. Length of hairs on terga respect MOD as follows: T2 0.22–0.54, T3 0.28–0.56, T4 0.51–0.84, T5 0.71–1.07. Sternites with simple hairs on apical and subapical areas.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 56–61. Female of
X. ( N. ) griswoldi
sp. nov.
56. Frontal view; 57. Mandible left. 58. Lateral habitus; 59. Dorsal habitus. 60. Basitibial plate; 61. T2 to T3.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Male : Body length 25.51 mm (23.95–27.19; n=3), length of forewing 20.01 mm (19.29–20.42; n=3), head length 4.55 mm (4.39–4.78; n=3), intertegular distance 6.59 mm (6.23–6.91; n=3), width of T2 11.46 mm (11.10– 12.09; n=3). Head : Mandibles bidentate; apical tooth longer and wider than internal. Labrum with a tubercle on the middle region. Lateral ocelli above the ocular margin; frontal groove well defined, unpunctate, initating with diameter equal to the anterior ocellus, diminishing to end in a middle (not strong) elevation between the antennal sockets. Pits on sublateral areas posterior to the ocelli, of half the diameter of the ocelli. Vertex elevated above the upper margin of eyes.
Mesosoma : Hind
tibia, basitibial plate with a spiniform projection toward the middle region; tibial spur thin; internal distal extreme of posterior tibia as described in diagnosis ( Fig. 65 ). Metasoma : T1 with a vertical fold with foveate surface. Genitalia : Distal angle of the ventral section of the gonostylus, obtuse ( Fig. 119 ); basal angle at the base of lateral process of the gonostylus, obtuse, barely noticeble ( Fig. 118 ); tip of the lateral process ending on an oval as broad as shaft of the process ( Fig. 118 ). Color : Integument in general yellowish-orange. Head : Integument brown light to dark on margins of labrum, clypeus, subantenal sutures, and posterior margin of antennae.
Mesosoma
: yellowish on anterior and lateral areas of tegulae and sclerite of wings. Mesepisternum and mesoesternal area black. Legs, coxae, and femurs brawn light to dark. Wings light brown (or orange); veins light brown to reddish. Metasoma : T1 to T6 with yellowish-orange hairs; T7 with more light hairs than yellowish ones. Length of hairs on terga respect MOD as follows: T2 0.41–0.92, T3 0.73–1.31, T4 0.89–1.31, T5 1.01–1.32. Sternites with light brown distal margin. Punctures : Head : Labrum: With wide subtriangular middle area unpunctate; submarginal and marginal areas unpuctate; rest of labrum with scattered punctures. Clypeus: with narrow middle longitudinal line unpunctate to the discal region which has few punctures; rest of clypeus with densely punctate separated by one and a half times or more their diameter; supraclipeal, paraocular, and genal areas with dense similar sized punctures; area unpunctate on sublateral region of the posterior ocelli. Vertex with area unpunctate on middle region, the rest densely punctate.
Mesosoma : Scutum
unpunctate on discal area; between the parapsidal line and discal area densely punctate, separated by one to one and a half times their diameter. Scutellum with small punctures on discal area, in contrast to lateral and posterior regions which have dense punctures. Mesepisternum and mesoesternal area densely punctate. Tegulae mostly without punctures, but with dense punctures on the anterior region. Propodeum with dense shallow punctures. Metasoma : Terga with dense uniform punctures. Discal area with punctures separated by one to one and a half times their diameter. Pubescence : Hind tibia: internal surface with long somewhat sparse hairs on basal region; internal anterior margin with long hairs, denser on apical region; internal posterior margin with long and short pubescence ( Fig. 65 ).
Etymology . This species is dedicated to Dr. Terry Griswold, a good friend, in recognition of his work as bee taxonomist. Also, for the comments and suggestions expressed during this study.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 62–65. Male of
X. ( N. ) griswoldi
sp. nov.
62. Frontal view; 63. Lateral habitus; 64. Dorsal habitus; 65. Hind tibia.

Commentaries . Several studies ( Ayala 1996 ; Minckley 1988; Mawdsley 2017 ) have erroneously identified specimens of this species as belonging to
X. mexicanorum
. It may be distinguished from
X. mexicanorum
by its convex and rounded scutellum in lateral view as well as its wing color, with magenta iridescence, bluish toward the base and papillate region throughout its distribution. According to our study
X. griswoldi
and
X. gualanensis
females differ in the punctation density on the discal area of T 2 ( Fig. 61 ) while males differ in the clypeal mid-line and the distal process of the inner surface of the hind tibia ( Fig. 65 ).
